items.title: Wave — A Texture Study
items.description: An impasto study of a single curling wave, painted with thick palette-knife strokes against a soft sky-blue gradient. The piece is about feeling the weight of a moment of water before it falls — built up in layers so that the paint itself ridges and breaks like spray.

items.title: Design Matrix — Elements × Principles
items.description: A wall-sized matrix mapping the seven elements of design (Line, Shape, Form, Color, Volume, Texture, Space) against the seven principles (Balance, Movement, Repetition, Contrast, Emphasis, Proportion, Unity) — and filling each cell with a real-world brand mark that demonstrates that combination. Hand-drawn, hand-painted, hand-cut. A small atlas of how visual language actually works in the world.

items.title: Dyslexia-Friendly Typeface — Research & Letterforms
items.description:
A research-led typography brief: how do you design a typeface that is genuinely easier to read for someone with dyslexia? The poster opens with a short brief on dyslexia (a neurological learning difficulty that affects reading, spelling and decoding), benchmarks existing solutions, and lays out the project's own design principles — heavily weighted bottoms, asymmetrical letterforms, wider letter-and-word spacing, slab-serif style.

The lower half is the typeface itself: a complete a–z, each letter drawn on its own paper coin so it can be moved, compared and rearranged.
